name: kairu-ai
description: Local-first AI chat for Web3 development. Supports Ollama (local), Anthropic Claude, and OpenAI.

commands.title: Kairu: Setup AI (Provider, Model, API Key)
Kairu: Set AI Provider API Key
Kairu: Delete AI Provider API Key
Kairu: Generate Commit Message from Staged Changes
Kairu: Send Message to AI Chat
Kairu: Review Changes with AI (Security + Correctness)

kairu.ai.provider.description: AI provider to use for chat. OpenRouter gives access to free models (Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen) with a free account. Cloud providers store API keys encrypted in the OS keychain.

kairu.ai.model.description: Model name (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6 for Anthropic, gpt-4o for OpenAI, gemini-2.5-pro for Gemini). Leave empty to be prompted by the setup wizard.

kairu.ai.openaiCompatible.endpoint.description: Custom OpenAI-compatible API endpoint (e.g. self-hosted vLLM). Leave empty if not using.

kairu.ai.systemPrompt.default: You are Kairu, an AI engineer embedded in a Web3 IDE. You assist with Solidity, smart contract security, Foundry, EVM internals, and protocol architecture. You are precise, terse, and never speculate beyond what the code shows. You flag risks before suggesting fixes.
kairu.ai.systemPrompt.description: System prompt prepended to every conversation.

kairu.ai.maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens to generate per response.

kairu.ai.toolsEnabled.description: Allow the AI to call tools (read/edit files, run forge build/test, run Slither, fetch from Etherscan, etc.) during chat. Currently supported on Anthropic Claude. Disable to make the AI advisory-only.

kairu.ai.staticCompletions.enabled.description: Static (non-AI) ghost-text completions for common Solidity/Vyper patterns: pragma, SPDX, contract/function/mapping declarations, etc. Free, instant, deterministic. On by default.

kairu.ai.inlineCompletions.enabled.markdownDescription: AI-powered ghost-text completions (like Copilot, but BYO API key). **Costs API tokens per fire** — typically a few cents per active hour. Off by default. Static pattern completions (free) are on by default and cover common boilerplate. Toggle via 'Kairu: Toggle Inline AI Completions'.

kairu.ai.inlineCompletions.model.description: Model used for inline completions. Defaults to Claude Haiku for low latency. Override to use a different model (e.g. gpt-4o-mini for OpenAI, gemini-2.5-flash for Gemini).

kairu.ai.inlineCompletions.maxTokens.description: Max tokens per inline completion. Lower = faster + cheaper, but completions may be cut off.

kairu.ai.inlineCompletions.debounceMs.description: Delay in milliseconds after typing stops before firing a completion request. Higher = fewer requests, more deliberate suggestions.

kairu.ai.includeActiveFile.description: Automatically inject the active editor's file as context.
